name: Winston Lin
title: Product, platform, and operating work in practice
tagline: I work across cloud products, developer platforms, AI workflows, and business systems. Most of my work has been where product judgment, technical detail, and day-to-day execution all matter at once: platform decisions, workflow design, rollout work, and the tools around them. I also advise and invest in a small set of businesses.
meta_description: Case studies, writing, and practical tools from cloud products, developer platforms, AI workflows, and business systems.

intro_paragraphs: I’ve worked across cloud products, developer platforms, AI workflows, and business systems, and I also work with operators and founders on growth and service design.
experience_highlights: Principal Product Manager for OCI Functions, focused on developer experience and execution model
Previously led product work across OCI DevOps / CI/CD and adjacent platform systems
Built internal platform capabilities that shortened delivery cycles and reduced operational drag

intro: I'm based in San Francisco and work across cloud products, developer tools, and AI workflows. Most of my work has been in the part of the job where product decisions, technical detail, and day-to-day execution all matter at once.

I like work that gets concrete quickly: shaping product direction, tightening workflows, improving docs and handoffs, and building small tools when they help. I use AI pretty hands-on as part of that, usually with real files, scripts, and review loops instead of one-off prompting.
I'm comfortable working across engineers, operators, leadership, and business stakeholders. Outside the day job, I also advise and invest in small businesses and startups in the U.S. and Asia.
background: I grew up between Taipei and Orange County, which made me comfortable moving between different environments early.

I later earned a B.S. from UC Berkeley Haas and got more interested in the overlap between business and technology. I also spent a lot of time tutoring, mentoring, and working in lab assistant roles, and stayed involved after college as a mentor in the undergraduate program through 2025.
I was also deeply into competitive math and community volunteering. That mix of analytical work and helping people still shapes how I think and how I like to work.
personal: Outside of work, I like being outdoors, hiking around the Bay Area, and shooting photography, mostly on Fujifilm.

I grew up playing sports and still play basketball and volleyball when I can. More recently I’ve gotten into running and Barry's classes. I also like anime, especially Frieren and Your Lie in April, and spend time thinking about investing, real estate, and long-term bets on businesses.
